phaseTitle: Choose the direction of the circle
phaseSubtitle: Vote for the subject the circle should hold. The Firekeeper will reveal one question from the chosen subject.

subjects.label: Truth
subjects.description: What is real? What can we know? How do we distinguish truth from illusion?

questions.text: Is truth discovered or created?
Can a lie ever be more truthful than the truth?
Is absolute truth possible, or only perspective?
Does knowing the truth always make us freer?
Can someone be honest and still deceive?

subjects.label: Freedom
subjects.description: What does it mean to be free? Are we ever truly free? What constrains us?

questions.text: Can we be truly free while needing others?
Is freedom possible without responsibility?
Do limits create freedom or destroy it?
Are we ever free from ourselves?
Is choosing less a form of greater freedom?

subjects.label: Meaning
subjects.description: What gives life meaning? How do we create purpose?

questions.text: Is meaning something we find or something we create?
Can a life without purpose still be meaningful?
Does suffering give life meaning or take it away?
Is meaning personal or universal?
Does meaning require others, or can it exist alone?

subjects.label: Self
subjects.description: Who are we? What is the nature of identity?

questions.text: Is the self something stable or constantly changing?
Do we choose who we become or discover it?
Is being true to yourself always right?
Can you fully know yourself?
Is self-love clarity or illusion?

subjects.label: Morality
subjects.description: What is right and wrong? Where does morality come from?

questions.text: Is it ever right to do the wrong thing?
What matters more: intention or outcome?
Are people inherently good or shaped to be?
Does morality exist without consequences?
Is moral compromise necessary for survival?

subjects.label: Fear
subjects.description: What do we fear? What does fear teach us?

questions.text: Is fear a guide or an obstacle?
Can fear be a form of intelligence?
Is a fearless life a better life?
Does fear reveal truth or distort it?
Should we trust what we fear?

subjects.label: Connection
subjects.description: What binds us? What separates us?

questions.text: Can we ever truly understand another person?
Is connection discovered or built?
Does vulnerability create connection or risk it?
Is deep connection rare or avoided?
Can you be fully yourself and still belong?

subjects.label: Attention
subjects.description: Where does our attention go? What deserves it?

questions.text: Does what we pay attention to define who we are?
Is attention a form of control or surrender?
Can we choose what deserves our attention?
Is distraction a failure or a protection?
Does attention create reality?

subjects.label: Existence
subjects.description: What does it mean to exist? Why is there something rather than nothing?

questions.text: Is existence itself meaningful or neutral?
Do we exist independently or through others?
Is being enough, or do we need to become?
Does awareness define existence?
Is life something to understand or to experience?

subjects.label: Beauty
subjects.description: What is beauty? Why do we seek it?

questions.text: Is beauty truth made visible?
Can something be beautiful without being good?
Does beauty exist without an observer?
Is beauty in harmony or in contrast?
Why does beauty move us?

subjects.label: Silence
subjects.description: What does silence reveal? What does it conceal?

questions.text: Is silence empty or full?
Can silence say more than words?
Are we afraid of silence, or of what it shows us?
Is silence a kind of listening?
Does silence belong to us or to the world?

subjects.label: Presence
subjects.description: What does it mean to be fully present?

questions.text: Is presence a choice or a state?
Can we be present without forgetting ourselves?
Does presence require silence?
Is being present the same as being alive?
What pulls us out of the present?

subjects.label: Compassion
subjects.description: What does it mean to truly care for another?

questions.text: Is compassion natural or learned?
Can we be compassionate without understanding?
Does compassion require sacrifice?
Is self-compassion the root of all compassion?
Where does compassion end and pity begin?

subjects.label: Knowledge
subjects.description: What can we truly know? How do we know it?

questions.text: Is knowledge the same as understanding?
Can we know something without language for it?
Does more knowledge bring more peace, or less?
Is unlearning a kind of knowing?
What is the cost of knowing?

subjects.label: Wisdom
subjects.description: What separates wisdom from knowledge?

questions.text: Is wisdom earned only through suffering?
Can the young be wise?
Is wisdom knowing what to ignore?
Does wisdom require humility?
Is it wiser to act or to wait?

subjects.label: Clarity
subjects.description: What does it mean to see clearly?

questions.text: Is clarity simplicity, or distillation?
Can clarity be painful?
Does clarity come from thought or from stillness?
Is clarity the same as truth?
What blocks us from seeing clearly?

subjects.label: Dialogue
subjects.description: What makes a real conversation?

questions.text: Is dialogue possible without disagreement?
Can we hear someone we already disagree with?
Is silence part of dialogue?
Does dialogue change who we are?
What turns talk into dialogue?

subjects.label: Understanding
subjects.description: What does it mean to truly understand?

questions.text: Can we understand without agreeing?
Is understanding a feeling or a thought?
Does understanding always bring closeness?
Can we understand someone we have never been?
Is understanding ever complete?

subjects.label: Empathy
subjects.description: What does it mean to feel with another?

questions.text: Is empathy a gift or a burden?
Can we have too much empathy?
Is empathy possible without experience?
Where does empathy end and self-loss begin?
Can empathy be taught?

subjects.label: Passion
subjects.description: What does it mean to live with passion?

questions.text: Is passion a guide or a danger?
Can a life without passion be a full life?
Does passion clarify or distort?
Is passion the opposite of peace?
Can we choose what we are passionate about?

subjects.label: Creativity
subjects.description: Where does creativity come from?

questions.text: Is creativity discovery or invention?
Can creativity exist without limits?
Is everyone creative, or only some?
Does creativity require suffering?
Is creating an act of courage?

subjects.label: Energy
subjects.description: What animates us? Where does our energy come from?

questions.text: Is energy something we have or something we channel?
Can energy be wasted, or only redirected?
Does what we focus on grow our energy?
Is rest the opposite of energy or its source?
What drains us most without us noticing?

subjects.label: Vitality
subjects.description: What does it mean to feel alive?

questions.text: Is vitality the same as happiness?
Can we be alive without feeling alive?
Does vitality come from the body or the mind?
Is vitality a choice or a gift?
What makes a life feel fully lived?

subjects.label: Change
subjects.description: What does it mean to change? What stays the same?

questions.text: Can we change without losing ourselves?
Is change something that happens or something we do?
Does anything truly stay the same?
Is fear of change really fear of loss?
What is the smallest change that changes everything?

subjects.label: Time
subjects.description: What is time? How do we live with it?

questions.text: Is time a thing, or only a way of thinking?
Does time heal, or only distance?
Can we ever step out of time?
Is the present real, or always already past?
Does time give meaning or take it away?

subjects.label: Impermanence
subjects.description: How do we live knowing nothing lasts?

questions.text: Does impermanence make life precious or painful?
Can we love something without trying to keep it?
Is letting go the same as giving up?
What endures, if nothing is permanent?
Does accepting impermanence free us or sadden us?

subjects.label: Acceptance
subjects.description: What does it mean to accept what is?

questions.text: Is acceptance peace, or surrender?
Can we accept what we cannot understand?
Does acceptance mean we stop trying to change?
Is accepting yourself harder than accepting others?
What is the difference between acceptance and resignation?
